what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry in the channel-memory ledger, mapping an attachment id to a target image URI under the design-assets channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields are present
standouts: Target URI points to a specific icon asset: image://design-assets/icons/Users3
File is purely metadata with no explanatory prose
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields
References an image asset via a custom image:// URI
Stores an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
concerns: No link back to the parent channel-memory record this attachment belongs to
Target URI lacks extension or version information
No description, title, or author context is provided
No indication of why this image is attached or its intended use
notes: A bare attachment ledger stub that ties an id to a design-assets icon image via a custom URI.
questions: Which channel-memory record does this attachment belong to?
Is the image:// URI resolvable and versioned?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
intention_signals: It appears intended to associate a specific icon image (Users3) with a design-assets channel memory so the asset can be retrieved or displayed alongside that memory.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme for the target and a file that contains only YAML frontmatter with no body.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable attachment record that links an identifier to a design-assets image target and a capture timestamp, preserving a provenance/relationship fact for fusion.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (id att-ae6fac45) in the channel-memory ledger that declares an images attachment targeting image://design-assets/icons/Users3 for the design-assets channel, created at 2026-06-22T12:26:24Z.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to record that a specific icon image (Users3) is attached to a design-assets channel memory entry.
